description:
Replace hardcoded default model selection in `internal/harnesses/codex/runner.go` and `internal/harnesses/claude/runner.go` with cassette-backed `ModelDiscoverySnapshot` selection. Explicit `--model <id>` pins still win.

## Current state

- `internal/harnesses/registry.go` embeds model discovery snapshots for Codex and Claude.
- `internal/harnesses/types.go` defines `ModelDiscoverySnapshot` with `Models []string`, `ReasoningLevels []string`, and `FreshnessWindow`.
- `internal/modelcatalog/tiers.go` from `fizeau-b2c5c826` provides the version-aware ranker: `modelcatalog.Parse(id)` returns `ParsedModel`, and `ParsedModel.Compare(other)` orders same-family models newest and strongest first.
- `internal/modelcatalog/catalog.go` exposes `Catalog.ModelEligibility(id)` and `ModelEntry.AutoRoutable()` for catalog IDs and declared surface IDs.
- The embedded catalog maps Codex model IDs on `modelcatalog.SurfaceCodex` and Claude Code model IDs on `modelcatalog.SurfaceClaudeCode`.

## Problem

Codex and Claude runner defaults are still hardcoded. Adding a newer auto-routable model should require only discovery/catalog evidence, not another runner code edit.

## Scope

1. Add an in-process discovery snapshot cache in `internal/harnesses/discovery_cache.go`, keyed by harness name and snapshot source. For this bead, the source is the embedded cassette snapshot already present in the registry; no live PTY refresh is required.
2. When no operator `--model` pin is provided, each runner loads the cached snapshot, filters `snapshot.Models` through `Catalog.ModelEligibility(modelID)`, keeps only `AutoRoutable == true`, sorts same-family candidates with `modelcatalog.Parse(...).Compare(...)`, and chooses the highest-ranked candidate.
3. Use `modelcatalog.SurfaceCodex` for Codex runner catalog projection and `modelcatalog.SurfaceClaudeCode` for Claude runner catalog projection. The concrete discovery strings `gpt-5.5` and `opus-4.7` are valid catalog surface IDs today.
4. When an explicit `--model <id>` pin is provided, keep using that exact pin. Discovery may warn if the pin is absent from the snapshot, but must not override it.
5. Surface a structured runner/default-resolution signal when the resolved default differs from the prior hardcoded default, so operators can see when discovery changes the selected model.
6. If snapshot lookup fails, fall back to the prior hardcoded default and emit a warning. Do not fail runner construction on discovery errors.

## Non-scope

- ReasoningLevels wiring; that is `fizeau-b0366923`.
- Persistent on-disk discovery storage.
- Live PTY refresh or background goroutine refresh.
- Cross-harness selection or cross-family ranking.

## Expected embedded defaults

Using the current embedded snapshots and catalog:

- Codex no-pin default resolves to `gpt-5.5`.
- Claude no-pin default resolves to `opus-4.7`.
- Codex explicit `--model gpt-5.4` remains `gpt-5.4`.

Add a small parser + ranker primitive in `internal/modelcatalog/tiers.go`
that orders model IDs within a family by (version, tier) so harness
discovery results can be sorted "newest most powerful first." Foundation
for bead B (wire harness discovery into runner as model authority).

## Scope

1. New file `internal/modelcatalog/tiers.go`:

```go
type Tier int
const (
    TierSmart    Tier = 0  // unsuffixed gpt, opus claude, pro gemini
    TierStandard Tier = 1  // mini gpt, sonnet claude, flash gemini
    TierCheap    Tier = 2  // nano gpt, haiku claude, flash-lite gemini
    TierUnknown  Tier = 99
)

var FamilyTiers = map[string]map[string]Tier{
    "gpt":    {"": TierSmart, "mini": TierStandard, "nano": TierCheap},
    "claude": {"opus": TierSmart, "sonnet": TierStandard, "haiku": TierCheap},
    "gemini": {"pro": TierSmart, "flash": TierStandard, "flash-lite": TierCheap},
}

type ParsedModel struct {
    Family     string  // "gpt", "claude", "gemini", ...
    Version    []int   // [5, 5] for gpt-5.5; [4, 7] for opus-4.7; normalized so [5] -> [5, 0]
    Tier       Tier    // smart/standard/cheap/unknown
    PreRelease bool    // true for "preview", "alpha", etc.
    Raw        string  // original ID
}

func Parse(id string) ParsedModel
func (a ParsedModel) Compare(b ParsedModel) int  // returns 0 across families
```

2. Lexicographic version comparison. `[5,5] > [5,4] > [5,3]`. Missing
   minor segments normalized to 0 on parse so `[5]` and `[5,0]` compare
   equal.

3. Cross-family Compare returns 0 (no opinion). Use catalog `power`
   field for cross-family ranking.

4. Pre-release suffix detection: `preview`, `alpha`, `beta`, `rc`. GA
   beats pre-release of the same version.

5. Unknown tier suffix (e.g. `gpt-5.3-codex-spark`) parses to
   `TierUnknown`. Sort order: TierUnknown ranks LOWER than all known
   tiers. The model is still returnable for explicit pin or fallback
   pool routing (downstream concern, not this bead).

In `internal/modelcatalog/tiers_test.go`:

```

gpt-5.5-mini        → {gpt,    [5,5],   TierStandard, pre=false}
gpt-5.5-nano        → {gpt,    [5,5],   TierCheap,    pre=false}
gpt-5.4             → {gpt,    [5,4],   TierSmart,    pre=false}
gpt-5.4-mini        → {gpt,    [5,4],   TierStandard, pre=false}
gpt-5               → {gpt,    [5,0],   TierSmart,    pre=false}    # normalized
gpt-5.5-preview     → {gpt,    [5,5],   TierSmart,    pre=true}
gpt-5.3-codex-spark → {gpt,    [5,3],   TierUnknown,  pre=false}
claude-opus-4-7     → {claude, [4,7],   TierSmart,    pre=false}
claude-sonnet-4-6   → {claude, [4,6],   TierStandard, pre=false}

```

Sort orderings:

sort([gpt-5.5-mini, gpt-5.5, gpt-5.5-nano, gpt-5.4]) ==

sort([gpt-5.5-preview, gpt-5.5]) == [gpt-5.5, gpt-5.5-preview]

## Catalog monotonicity invariants

Extend `internal/modelcatalog/embedded_power_test.go` to assert:
- For each (family, tier) cohort in the embedded catalog: `power` is

Build fails if catalog values violate these. Cross-family comparisons

## Out of scope

- Wiring into routing or harness runners (bead B).
- ReasoningLevels parsing (bead C).
- IncludeByDefault filter (bead D).
- quota_pool catalog field (bead E1).
- Cross-family ranking (catalog power's job, not the ranker's).

description:
PROBLEM
  internal/routing engine carries legacy Profile field naming, treats MinPower/MaxPower as hard candidate filters, threads a ModelRef field the v0.11 surface no longer uses, and resolves catalog refs through Inputs.CatalogResolver callbacks the service layer no longer needs. ErrNoLiveProvider.StartingTier is a field-name bug — it always held the policy name.

ROOT CAUSE
  Engine predates the v0.11 routing-surface redesign. "Profile" was the canonical routing input, "model-ref" was a kitchen-sink string accepting policies/targets/aliases, and power bounds were enforced as exclusion gates rather than scoring signals.

DESIGN INTENT (v0.11)
  - "Policy" is the single canonical word for the named routing-intent bundle. No aliases.
  - ModelRef removed; callers pass concrete req.Model when pinning, otherwise rely on req.Policy.
  - MinPower/MaxPower preserved on the request but become asymmetric scoring weights, NOT filters. Undershoot 5x worse than overshoot: power_score = -5*max(0, min_power - cand.power) - 1*max(0, cand.power - max_power).
  - New per-policy bool req.AllowLocal controls whether local-billing candidates are included. Smart's allow_local=false produces fail-closed when subscription is dry (no candidates → ErrNoLiveProvider).
  - require[] is a list of hard-constraint predicates applied as candidate filter. v0.11 ships with one: "no_remote" (cand.DeploymentClass == "local").
  - Pin-pin conflicts (harness×model, harness×provider, provider×model) fail with ErrUnsatisfiablePin BEFORE scoring.

CURRENT STATE WITH FILE:LINE
  - internal/routing/engine.go:16-34 Request struct: Profile, ModelRef, MinPower/MaxPower fields
  - internal/routing/engine.go:284-308 ErrNoLiveProvider.StartingTier (field-name bug)
  - internal/routing/engine.go:305 ProfileEscalationLadder
  - internal/routing/engine.go:1175-1209 ModelRef-driven catalog resolution branch
  - internal/routing/engine.go:1229-1257 EscalateProfileAware
  - internal/routing/errors.go:11-15,46-98 errProfilePinConflict, errNoProfileCandidate sentinels
  - internal/routing/score.go:28,36,58-101 scorePolicy switch on profile name strings
  - internal/routing/gating.go pin-collision predicates

PROPOSED FIX
  1. Rename Request.Profile → Request.Policy. Add Request.AllowLocal bool, Request.Require []string. Drop Request.ModelRef.
  2. Add asymmetric soft-power scoring in score.go (formula above).
  3. Add pin-pin compatibility predicate in gating.go. Apply Require[] predicates as candidate filter. Only "no_remote" defined in v0.11.
  4. Delete Inputs.CatalogResolver and Inputs.CatalogCandidatesResolver fields. Drop the ModelRef branch in resolveModel (engine.go:1175-1209).
  5. errors.go: replace ErrProfilePinConflict, ErrNoProfileCandidate with single ErrPolicyRequirementUnsatisfied{Policy, Requirement, AttemptedPin string, Rejected int}. Add ErrUnknownPolicy{Policy string}, ErrUnsatisfiablePin{Pin, Reason string}.
  6. Rename ErrNoLiveProvider.StartingTier → StartingPolicy. Rename ProfileEscalationLadder → PolicyEscalationLadder; values ["cheap", "default", "smart"].
  7. Rename EscalateProfileAware → EscalatePolicyAware. Internal helpers: missingProfileCapability → missingPolicyCapability; explicitProfileConstraint → explicitPolicyConstraint; harnessViolatesProfileConstraint, modelPinViolatesProfileConstraint similarly.
  8. score.go switch cases canonicalized: "cheap" / "default" / "smart". Format strings "profile=%s" → "policy=%s".

NON-SCOPE
  - Public service surface (service*.go) — Phase 4.
  - Catalog manifest schema — Phase 2.
  - CLI flags — Phase 6.
  - DDx coordination — Phase 9.

PARENT + DEPS
  Leads phase chain. Phase 2 depends on this.
  9. ErrNoLiveProvider enhancement: in addition to renaming StartingTier→StartingPolicy, add RejectedCandidates []CandidateRejection field. description:
Killing a sweep parent (run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh PID) does not currently kill its descendant matrix-runner / harbor children. They are reparented to init (PPID=1) and keep executing. Concrete failures this session:

1. **2026-05-11 ~22:30Z** — Killed the local-lane sweep (PID 463900). Vidar matrix-runner child kept running on the dead oMLX server, wasted 70 cells with retry-spam pattern before I noticed.

2. **2026-05-11 ~18:15Z** — Killed the timing-baseline ds4 sweep (PID 942197) after the reasoning-not-supported bug. The matrix-runner became orphan (945491, PPID=1). Then I launched a replacement (991522 → 999500) and a second iteration (975660, orphan). All three were running simultaneously against ds4's single graph worker, contending for the slot and writing to the same report.json paths. Smoke tests timed out because of slot contention. Took ~30 min to notice and clean up.

The general pattern: any time we `kill -KILL <sweep_pid>` to restart with new config (multiplier change, profile fix, server-side issue), one or more children survive. They consume identical work, race the new sweep, and corrupt the per-cell results.

PROPOSED FIX

Two interlocking pieces:

A. **run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh: trap signals + propagate.** Add a trap handler in the shell script that on SIGTERM/SIGINT/EXIT:
   - Records the matrix-runner PIDs as they spawn
   - On signal, sends SIGTERM to each matrix-runner PID; if still alive after 10s, SIGKILL
   - Then exits

B. **cmd/bench (sweep + matrix subcommands): handle SIGTERM by killing harbor children.** When fiz-bench receives SIGTERM, propagate to in-flight `harbor run` subprocesses (exec.CommandContext already supports this via context cancellation). The existing `cmd.Cancel = func() error { return cmd.Process.Signal(syscall.SIGTERM) }` is correct shape; verify it actually fires on parent SIGTERM and not just on context.WithTimeout.

C. **Alternative (single-knob fix): process groups.** Have the sweep script start fiz-bench in its own process group (`setpgid`), and have the kill path send SIGTERM to the entire process group (`kill -TERM -<pgid>`). One signal takes out the whole tree atomically. Simpler than A+B; downside is no graceful per-process shutdown order.

In-scope files:
- scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh — trap handler + PID tracking
- cmd/bench/sweep.go and cmd/bench/matrix.go — signal handling on the Go side (verify existing exec.CommandContext + cmd.Cancel work as intended on SIGTERM, not just context cancel)
- Tests / integration: confirm a `kill -TERM $sweep_pid` reaps the entire process tree within 30s.

Out-of-scope:
- Persistent state recovery (sweep resume from interrupted cell). Already handled by --resume + report.json being the durable state.
- Distinguishing "user-initiated kill" from "supervisor-initiated kill" — both should cleanly shut down.

ACCEPTANCE IS A COMMAND

1. `bash scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh --phase canary --lanes ds4 &` then `kill $!` results in ZERO surviving fiz-bench or harbor processes within 30s (verify via `pgrep -f "fiz-bench|harbor run"`).

2. Same scenario but `kill -TERM` — the shutdown is graceful: each in-flight cell's harbor invocation gets SIGTERM (lets it tear down its docker-compose stack via the existing `--delete` finalizer, see cmd/bench/matrix.go cmd.Cancel handler), waits up to 60s for it to finish, then exits.

3. New integration test scripts/benchmark/test_sweep_shutdown.bash spawns a sweep, sends SIGTERM, asserts (a) all sweep-related processes are gone within 60s, (b) report.json files for in-flight cells are either complete or absent (no half-written state).
notes: Operator made ACs mechanically verifiable with explicit test names and cleared stale AC-quality gate metadata.
title: bench: clean shutdown — kill -TERM sweep reaps the whole process tree

description:
Add an optional `quota_pool: <name>` field to ModelEntry in the catalog
manifest. Foundation for ADR-011 (bead E2) cost-based routing — models
that share a quota allocation share a pool name; models with
independent quotas (e.g. gpt-5.3-codex-spark) get their own pool.

This bead is schema-only. No routing consumers yet. Lands as preparation
so ADR-011 can reference a real field.

## Scope

1. Add `QuotaPool string` field to ModelEntry in
   `internal/modelcatalog/manifest.go`. YAML tag `quota_pool,omitempty`.
2. Default semantic when unset: model's quota pool == its provider
   system name (e.g., openai-hosted models share the "openai" pool by
   default; ds4 models share "ds4"). Documented in the field's GoDoc.
3. Add a helper `ModelEntry.EffectiveQuotaPool() string` that returns
   the explicit pool if set, else the default-derived pool.
4. Round-trip test: a manifest with `quota_pool: openai-codex-spark` on
   a model entry round-trips through Load and serialization without
   loss.
5. Validator: pool name must be non-empty when set; characters
   restricted to lowercase, digits, dash (no spaces, no special chars).

## Catalog updates included in this bead

- `gpt-5.3-codex-spark` (if it exists in the catalog; else add it):

add it — that's an operator decision. Just exercise the field on any
existing entry.

## In-scope files

## Out of scope

- Per-model pricing fields (already exist).

description:
DEFERRED. Filed proposed-status for future work.

OpenRouter routes a single model (e.g. qwen/qwen3.6-27b) to multiple
upstream sub-providers (DeepInfra, Together, Fireworks, etc.). Each
has its own pricing, latency, context window, and rate limits. For
high-fidelity routing, the snapshot should distinguish them.

## Syntax (per ADR-012 naming convention)

`<provider>/<model>@<sub_provider>`. Examples:
- `openrouter/qwen/qwen3.6-27b@deepinfra`
- `openrouter/qwen/qwen3.6-27b@together`
- `openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6@anthropic`

## Source

OR's `/api/v1/models/<id>/endpoints` returns:
```
{
  "data": {
    "endpoints": [
      {"provider_name": "DeepInfra", "context_length": ..., ...},
      {"provider_name": "Together", "context_length": ..., ...}
    ]
  }
}
```

## Why deferred

- Per-OR-model API call (N+1 problem; OR has hundreds of models)
- Cardinality blowup in the snapshot (each model becomes N entries)
- Requires routing-layer changes to actually USE the sub-provider
  selection
- Marginal value for current benchmark-mode use case (we're not
  routing through OR at scale)

## Re-evaluate when

- We ship cost-based routing (ADR-011, bead E2) and quota signals
  start mattering across OR sub-providers
- We benchmark OR-Qwen3 across multiple upstream sub-providers and
  see measurable performance differences
- An operator explicitly requests it

## Depends on

- M2 (snapshot assembler) — extension, not parallel implementation

description:
PROBLEM
  Public service surface (service*.go, routing_errors.go, internal/serviceimpl, internal/core) exposes Profile naming, ModelRef fields, ResolveProfile/ProfileAliases methods, and a sprawling FizeauService interface (17 methods). The v0.11 surface trims this to ~14 methods, renames Profile→Policy, drops ModelRef entirely, and removes target-language from comments/error strings.

ROOT CAUSE
  Public surface predates v0.11. Profile/ModelRef were the canonical inputs; ResolveProfile returned per-surface preview data tied to the dropped Surface concept. Target language is sprinkled through service_models.go and other files as legacy of the dropped target abstraction.

DESIGN INTENT (v0.11)
  - FizeauService interface methods: rename ListProfiles→ListPolicies; drop ResolveProfile and ProfileAliases entirely.
  - Types: ProfileInfo→PolicyInfo (no AliasOf field, no Target field); drop ResolvedProfile, ProfileSurface entirely.
  - Request fields: Profile→Policy on RouteRequest and ServiceExecuteRequest. Add AllowLocal bool, Require []string. Drop ModelRef from RouteRequest, ServiceExecuteRequest, and ResolvedModelRef field. Keep MinPower/MaxPower as soft signals.
  - RouteDecision.RequestedProfile→RequestedPolicy. RoutePowerPolicy{Profile, MinPower, MaxPower}→RoutePowerPolicy{PolicyName, MinPower, MaxPower}.
  - Public errors: ErrProfilePinConflict/ErrNoProfileCandidate/ErrUnknownProfile→ErrPolicyRequirementUnsatisfied + ErrUnknownPolicy + ErrUnsatisfiablePin (mirroring Phase 1 routing errors).
  - HarnessInfo: drop IsLocal/IsSubscription bools, replace with Billing modelcatalog.BillingModel.
  - ProviderInfo: add IncludeByDefault bool, Billing modelcatalog.BillingModel.
  - ModelInfo: add Billing modelcatalog.BillingModel; drop CatalogRef field.
  - Scrub all routing-context "target" language from production code (comments, error strings, helper function names like catalogRefForModel). Add scoped lint rule preventing reintroduction in service_*.go, internal/routing/, internal/modelcatalog/.

  - service.go:228-249 HarnessInfo (IsLocal/IsSubscription)
  - service.go:260-313 ProviderInfo, ModelInfo

PROPOSED FIX

Update all request/response types (RouteRequest, RouteDecision, RoutePowerPolicy, ServiceExecuteRequest, HarnessInfo, ProviderInfo, ModelInfo) per design intent.
  2. Rename service_profiles.go → service_policies.go. Rewrite: implement ListPolicies returning []PolicyInfo from cat.Policies(). Drop everything else.

description:
The `ProviderConfig.IncludeByDefault *bool` field at
`internal/config/config.go:48` is plumbed to ServiceConfig at
`internal/config/serviceconfig.go:73` but has NO consumer in
`internal/routing/`. Half-built: operators can set
`include_by_default: false` in their config, the value is captured, but
routing ignores it.

This bead wires the field into routing's eligibility filter so the
operator's intent is honored.

## Scope

1. In `internal/routing/gating.go` (or the equivalent eligibility
   filter), add a check: if the candidate's provider has
   `IncludeByDefault == false` AND the request did not explicitly pin
   that provider, mark the candidate as ineligible with reason
   `provider_excluded_from_default_routing`.

2. Explicit pin paths (`--provider <name>`, `--harness <name> --model
   <id>` resolved to that provider) bypass the filter.

3. Surface the exclusion in route-status output so operators can see
   `provider X eligible=false reason=excluded_from_default_routing`.

4. Add regression tests covering:
   - `include_by_default: false` + unpinned request → provider excluded
   - `include_by_default: false` + explicit `--provider X` → provider
     selected
   - `include_by_default: true` (default) → unchanged behavior

## In-scope files

- internal/routing/gating.go (or filter equivalent)
- internal/routing/gating_test.go (or equivalent)
- internal/config/config_test.go (extend if needed)

## Out of scope

  `manifest.go:126` `IncludeByDefault bool`).
- Quota-aware routing (bead E2).
- Per-model eligibility (catalog `ExactPinOnly` already covers this).

description:
Build a CLI at cmd/fizeau-probe-reasoning that snapshots provider
introspection AND verifies whether the upstream actually behaves the way
introspection claims. Per ADR-010 Amendment 2026-05-11, the probe is now
primarily a verification tool, not a discovery tool — introspection
adapters (fizeau-e80c156e) handle discovery where it's available.

Three modes of output per (provider, model):

1. **Snapshot** — verbatim capture of every introspection endpoint the
   provider exposes:
     ds4:           /props, /v1/models, /v1/models/<id>
     llama-server:  /props, /v1/models, /slots, /health, /metrics
     OR:            /api/v1/models, /api/v1/models/<id> if supported
     others:        whatever GET <base>/v1/models returns
   Written verbatim into the artifact directory as JSON files. This is
   the auditable record of \"what the server claimed on date X.\"

2. **Verification matrix** — send a small set of seeded chat completions
   that exercise each documented wire knob:
     - reasoning: off
     - reasoning: low (KindNamed, PortableBudgets=2048)
     - reasoning: medium (KindNamed, PortableBudgets=8192)
     - reasoning: high (KindNamed, PortableBudgets=32768)
     - reasoning: 4096 (KindTokens explicit)
     - reasoning: 16384 (KindTokens explicit)
   For each row capture: wire body sent, finish_reason,
   usage.completion_tokens_details.reasoning_tokens (if present), len of
   message.reasoning_content, wall time. Hash of <think> block content
   when present (so flat-mapping is visible).

   Use a stable single-prompt with seed pinning when the upstream supports
   it (sampling.seed). Prompt: \"What is 17 times 24? Show your work step
   by step.\" Small enough to run fast (~30-60s per request on local
   lanes); large enough to produce measurable thinking.

3. **Verdict** — one of:
     introspection-trusted: claims and observations match (e.g. ds4's
       alias map predicts low/medium/xhigh produce identical thinking
       depth, and the matrix confirms within sampling noise).
     introspection-divergent: claims and observations disagree. Print
       the disagreement and flag for operator review.
     introspection-absent: no useful introspection endpoint; catalog L2
       required. Print recommended `reasoning_wire` value.

CLI surface:
  fizeau-probe-reasoning --profile <path> [--artifact-dir <path>]
                          [--json] [--snapshot-only]
                          [--matrix-only] [--seed <int>]
  fizeau-probe-reasoning --provider <type> --base-url <url> --model <id>
                          [--api-key-env <var>] ...

Default artifact-dir: /tmp/fizeau-probe-<timestamp>/<provider>/

Run timeout per request: 5 minutes (long-thinking Qwen3 / DS4 can take
60s+ on local hardware).

Makefile: add `make probe-reasoning PROFILE=<path>` target.

In-scope files:
- cmd/fizeau-probe-reasoning/main.go (new)
- cmd/fizeau-probe-reasoning/snapshot.go (new) — introspection endpoint
  capture per provider type.
- cmd/fizeau-probe-reasoning/matrix.go (new) — verification matrix
  runner.
- cmd/fizeau-probe-reasoning/verdict.go (new) — claim-vs-observation
  diff logic.
- cmd/fizeau-probe-reasoning/probe_test.go (new) — table tests against
  recorded fixtures (no live endpoint needed for unit tests).
- Makefile — `probe-reasoning` target.

Out-of-scope:
- Auto-writing back to the catalog (operator reviews and writes
  manually).
- Sweeping multiple models in one invocation (shell loop is fine).
- Real tokenizer for reasoning_content length conversion (use chars/4
  approximation, matching the telemetry bead fizeau-8f62bcbb).

Depends on: fizeau-e80c156e (introspection adapters — probe uses the
same snapshot logic so the two share code).
title: Build fizeau-probe-reasoning CLI for wire-form measurement

description:
EPIC for the available-models snapshot architecture. The user-facing
deliverable is a `fiz models` CLI subcommand. The architectural
deliverable is the foundation for the auto-router: a runtime-resolved
view of "what can fizeau actually use right now," assembled from all
discovery sources, enriched with catalog metadata, surfaced through a
multi-process-safe disk cache.

## End goal

For a given fizeau install at a given time, there is a canonical set
of known models — one entry per (provider, model_id) — with defined
properties: identity, parsed family/version/tier, catalog-derived
power/cost/reasoning_levels/quota_pool, and runtime-derived status/
quota_remaining/recent latency. Operators introspect via `fiz models`;
routing reads the same snapshot to score candidates. Single source of
truth; no separate "what does routing think exists" vs "what does
operator see" divergence.

## Child beads (in dependency order)

- **M0** (ADR-012): per-source on-disk cache for discovery + runtime
  signals. Defines file layout, locking semantics, single-flight
  refresh algorithm, atomic-write contract, test matrix.
- **M1**: cache infrastructure — implements ADR-012's algorithm with
  the 10-test robustness matrix. Includes `ParseModelRef`/canonical
  identity helpers.
- **M2**: snapshot assembler — enumerate providers (incl harnesses-
  as-providers) → discover via cache → parse via tier-ranker
  (depends on routing bead A) → enrich from catalog (joins on
  power, cost, reasoning_levels, quota_pool from bead E1).
- **M3**: runtime signal collector — status, quota_remaining, recent
  p50 latency → runtime cache.
- **M4**: `fiz models` top-level CLI subcommand. Default filtered
  view; `--include-noise --refresh --no-refresh --json --provider X
  --power-min/max`. Detail view via `fiz models <ref>` with shortform
  fuzzy resolution and full-canonical exact match.
- **M5** (deferred): OR sub-provider expansion via `@<sub_provider>`
  syntax. Filed proposed-status; future work.

## Architecture invariants

1. **Identity = (provider, model_id).** Canonical form `<provider>/
   <model_id>`. Provider names slug-safe; model IDs preserved
   provider-side. Split-on-first-slash.
2. **Harness == provider** for snapshot purposes. Subscription harness
   wrappers participate as named providers (e.g.,
   `claude-subscription`, `codex-subscription`).
3. **Catalog is implementation detail.** No catalog-only entries
   appear in `fiz models`. If no provider serves a model, it's not
   "known."
4. **No aliases in snapshot.** Aliases are a search/discovery
   concern (CLI-side fuzzy resolution); snapshot stores canonical
   entries only.
5. **Per-source on-disk cache**, multi-process-safe, with strict
   100ms UI budget. Discovery cache long-TTL; runtime cache short-TTL.
6. **Single-flight refresh** across processes via PID+marker pattern
   (extending fizeau's existing `acquireMatrixLock` idiom).
7. **Stale-while-revalidate** by default. Force-refresh waits.
8. **Atomic write** via tmp+fsync+rename. No torn reads.

## Governing doc updates

- **NEW: ADR-012** — per-source disk cache for discovery + runtime
- **FEAT-006** (standalone CLI) — add `fiz models` subcommand
- **FEAT-004** (model routing) — addendum: routing reads snapshot
- **ADR-009** (routing surface redesign) — addendum: harness-as-
  provider in snapshot

## Closes when

All five active children (M0, M1, M2, M3, M4) closed. M5 is filed
proposed-status as future work and does not block this epic.
title: fiz models — assembled snapshot per (provider, model) for routing + introspection

description:

Introspection adapters read provider /props at construction time and
override the static ThinkingFormat default with what the live server
actually claims.

Per ADR-010 Amendment 2026-05-11 the L1 layer (live introspection) is the
authoritative source where available; this bead implements L1 for ds4 and
llama-server. The static defaults that fizeau-073fe18b sets become the L3
floor; L2 (catalog reasoning_wire) sits in between.

After this bead lands, fizeau auto-detects:
- ds4's alias map → effective reasoning_levels without catalog curation
- llama-server's chat_template variant → auto-detect kwargs vs top-level
  envelope (today's wire choice is hardcoded by fizeau-073fe18b)

Cost model (measured 2026-05-11):
  ds4 /props on LAN:         p50=13ms  max=51ms  (n=10)
  sindri /props on LAN:      p50=9ms   max=13ms  (n=10)
  OR /api/v1/models cloud:   p50=316ms max=325ms (n=5)

Three adapters in this bead:

1. **ds4 adapter** (internal/provider/ds4/introspection.go, new):
   GET http://<base>/props. Parse reasoning.{supported_efforts, aliases,
   default, effective_default, think_max_min_context},
   api.supported_request_parameters, sampling, runtime. Derive effective
   ThinkingFormat=OpenAIEffort and effective reasoning_levels from
   supported_efforts minus aliased duplicates.

2. **llama-server adapter** (internal/provider/llamaserver/introspection.go,
   new): GET http://<base>/props. Parse
   default_generation_settings.params.reasoning_format, chat_template

   chat_template_caps.supports_preserve_reasoning, build_info.

3. **Common plumbing** (internal/provider/registry/introspection.go, new):
   ProviderIntrospection struct, registry of adapters by provider type,
   cache by (base_url, model), non-fatal failure with structured warning.

Construction order: instantiate provider → run introspection adapter (if
registered) → merge into capabilities → ready. Per-request path is
unchanged; no introspection IO at request time.

New fixture testdata/matrix-invalid/no-llm-response.json + test case asserts classification as invalid_provider. 2. scripts/benchmark/reclassify-invalid.py --dry-run on existing history flags the 54+ known no-response cells transitioning from '' to 'invalid_provider'. 3. description:
Currently when fiz makes an llm.request and gets no llm.response back (cloud-side hang, 5xx, stream-aborted, rate-limit drop, etc.), the cell ends up as a normal graded_fail with reward=0, turns=0, terminated_mid_work=None. The matrix runner treats it identically to a real model failure — even though no actual model attempt happened. Result: 54 of OR-Qwen's 239 "failures" (22%) on TB-2.1 are this class, polluting the pass-rate denominator and wasting the cell entirely.

Two complementary fixes:

A. **Matrix-side classifier rule** (low-effort, high-impact):
   In cmd/bench/matrix_invalid.go, add: if final_status="graded_fail" AND
   (turns is nil OR turns==0) AND terminated_mid_work is nil AND (input_tokens
   is nil OR input_tokens==0), classify as `invalid_provider`. The existing
   `--retry-invalid` flag will then re-run these on the next sweep pass.
   Mirrors the same structural-rule pattern as the recent harness_crash →
   invalid_setup and ran-no-attempt → invalid_setup additions.

B. **In-fiz request-level retry** (deeper, may belong in a separate bead):
   When fiz issues an llm.request and gets no streamed tokens within
   provider_timeout, retry with exponential backoff (e.g. 1s, 4s, 16s)
   up to 3 attempts before failing the turn. Should be capped by total
   wall budget. This stops the request from ever reaching "no llm.response"
   state in the first place. Requires care to avoid amplifying real
   server-side stalls into runaway retries.

Acceptance is a command:

1. With a stub provider that returns HTTP 200 with empty body (simulating
   the no-response state), `go test ./cmd/bench/... -run TestClassifyMatrixInvalid`
   has a new fixture report (`no-llm-response.json`) with the structural
   shape above, asserted to classify as `invalid_provider`.
2. `scripts/benchmark/reclassify-invalid.py --dry-run` on the existing
   3700+ historical reports flags the 54 known OR-Qwen no-response cells
   plus any other lanes' equivalents as transitioning from `""` to
   `invalid_provider`. After apply, those cells are excluded from the
   pass@k denominator and tagged for `--retry-invalid` reruns.
3. (Optional, defer to Part B bead) `internal/core/loop_test.go` covers
   the retry-on-empty-response behavior with a stub provider that yields
   nothing on the first 2 attempts and a normal response on the 3rd.

In-scope files for Part A (this bead):
- cmd/bench/matrix_invalid.go — add the classifier branch
- cmd/bench/matrix_invalid_test.go — add fixture test
- cmd/bench/testdata/matrix-invalid/no-llm-response.json — new fixture
- scripts/benchmark/reclassify-invalid.py — mirror the Go logic
- scripts/benchmark/backfill-terminated-mid-work.py (already handles
  this case via the structural rule but not the classifier tag)

Out-of-scope:
- Part B (in-fiz request retry) — file as a separate bead if/when needed.
- Distinguishing "5xx" vs "rate-limit" vs "timeout" — at the report.json
  level we just see "no llm.response"; downstream telemetry could refine
  but this bead just gets us to "retriable cell".
- Adjusting harbor's own retry behavior.

Why this matters for the QWEN_BASE experiment:
The OR-Qwen 2.5x rerun experiment (in flight as of 2026-05-11 15:29Z) will
re-run the 239 previously-failed cells. If 22% of those originally failed
with "no llm.response", we expect roughly the same fraction to hit the
same class on the rerun. This bead would catch them with `--retry-invalid`
on a subsequent pass instead of leaving them as silent failures.
title: bench: tag 'no llm.response' cells as invalid_provider so --retry-invalid catches them

The official medium/frontier TerminalBench matrix must stop invoking raw Harbor Claude, Codex, pi, or opencode adapters and must use one Harbor installed agent, scripts/benchmark/harbor_agent.py:FizeauAgent. Child beads should land the CLI harness pin, FizeauAgent env pass-through for all fiz-wrapped harnesses, profile/script rewrite, invalid-run classification, docs/tests, and final canary validation.

Out of scope: making raw scripts/benchmark/harbor_adapters/claude.py, codex.py, pi.py, or opencode.py production quality, adding matrix concurrency, changing TerminalBench scoring, or claiming OpenRouter Sonnet and Claude Code Sonnet are identical provider surfaces.
